name: Crisp Chicken- Szechuan
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine Peppersalt and sherry.
2. Pound scallions with the broad side of a cleaver, then chop into 1-inch lengths. Add to the Peppersalt mixture.
3. Rinse chicken in cold water, then pat dry with paper towels. Rub the inside and outside of the chicken with the peppercorn mixture. Refrigerate for 8 hours.
4. Pour 4 cups boiling water into a wok or large pot. Place the chicken in a baking dish in a steamer or on a steamer rack over the boiling water. Cover and steam the chicken over medium heat for about 2 hours, until the meat is very tender. Add more boiling water as needed.
5. Remove the wok from the heat and let cool slightly. Remove the chicken and pat dry. Let it cool, then brush soy sauce all over the chicken.
6. Combine flour and cornstarch in a shallow dish. Dust the chicken with the mixture.
7. Heat oil in a wok to 350°F. Carefully lower the chicken into hot oil using tongs. Deep fry for 2 minutes. Remove the chicken from the oil using tongs and set aside.
8. Reheat the oil to 350°F, then carefully submerge the chicken again. Deep fry for 1 minute or until the chicken is browned and crisp. Remove and let cool a few minutes.
9. Use a heavy, sharp cleaver to chop the cooked chicken into small serving pieces (4 servings).
10. To prepare Peppersalt, heat a medium saucepan over medium-low heat for 1 minute. Add Szechuan peppercorns and stir-fry for 5 minutes. Remove from heat and let cool. Grind the peppercorns into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle. Add salt and mix well. Store in a tightly covered container.
11. Makes about 1 cup.
